levels.title: Identification & Form
levels.miniLesson:
🎯 Level 1: The Core Rules

ArticleRuleExample
A / AnSingular, countable, 1st mention.She read an article.
TheSpecific, known to reader.She read the article.
Zero (∅)General plural/uncountable.∅ Research takes time.

questionBank.question: The university built ___ new library for the science department.
questionBank.explanation: Use 'a' because 'library' is a singular countable noun mentioned for the first time.

questionBank.question: ___ information provided in the lecture was difficult to understand.
questionBank.explanation: Use 'The' because it refers to specific information from that specific lecture.

questionBank.question: Many international students find that studying ___ English takes years of practice.
questionBank.explanation: Languages do not take an article when spoken about generally.

questionBank.question: The professor asked us to write ___ essay about climate change.
questionBank.explanation: Use 'an' before a singular noun that starts with a vowel sound (essay).

questionBank.question: Where is ___ textbook I left on the desk yesterday?
questionBank.explanation: Use 'the' because the speaker is asking about a specific, known textbook.

questionBank.question: I need to buy ___ laptop before the semester begins.
questionBank.explanation: Use 'a' because the speaker means any laptop, not one specific laptop yet.

questionBank.question: ___ water is essential for all living organisms.
questionBank.explanation: Use the zero article for uncountable nouns (water) when speaking in general.

questionBank.question: Can you pass me ___ water bottle next to your bag?
questionBank.explanation: Use 'the' because it refers to a specific water bottle that both people can see.

questionBank.question: She is studying to become ___ engineer.
questionBank.explanation: Use 'a/an' when stating someone's profession. 'Engineer' starts with a vowel sound.

questionBank.question: ___ sun rises in the east and sets in the west.
questionBank.explanation: Use 'The' for unique objects; there is only one sun in our solar system.

questionBank.question: He has ___ hour to finish the final exam.
questionBank.explanation: Use 'an' because 'hour' starts with a vowel sound (the 'h' is silent).

questionBank.question: They are conducting a study on ___ behavior of dolphins.
questionBank.explanation: Use 'the' because it is specified by the prepositional phrase 'of dolphins'.

questionBank.question: ___ computers have drastically changed how we work.
questionBank.explanation: Use the zero article for plural countable nouns when speaking generally about the whole category.

questionBank.question: We visited ___ Louvre Museum while studying in Paris.
questionBank.explanation: Famous museums, galleries, and buildings usually require 'the'.

questionBank.question: I had ___ really good idea for my research project.
questionBank.explanation: Use 'a' for a singular countable noun ('idea') mentioned for the first time.

questionBank.question: The researchers found ___ solution to the problem.
questionBank.explanation: It is one of potentially many solutions, and is being introduced for the first time.

questionBank.question: ___ solution they proposed was rejected by the board.
questionBank.explanation: Use 'The' because it refers to the specific solution mentioned previously.

questionBank.question: Most people agree that ___ health is more important than wealth.
questionBank.explanation: Abstract uncountable nouns (health) take no article when speaking generally.

questionBank.question: The doctor was concerned about ___ health of the patient.
questionBank.explanation: It takes 'the' because it is specific: the health 'of the patient'.

questionBank.question: Do you have ___ pen I could borrow for the test?
questionBank.explanation: The speaker doesn't need a specific pen, just any single pen.

questionBank.question: Please return ___ pen I lent you yesterday.
questionBank.explanation: Use 'the' because it is a specific pen known to both speaker and listener.

questionBank.question: She applied to study at ___ University of Oxford.
questionBank.explanation: Universities with the structure 'University of [Name]' take 'the'.

questionBank.question: She applied to study at ___ Oxford University.
questionBank.explanation: Universities with the structure '[Name] University' generally do not take an article.

questionBank.question: Reading about ___ Nobel Prize in Chemistry inspired her to become a scientist.
questionBank.explanation: Named, specific awards are unique institutions and always take 'the'. As there is only one Nobel Prize in Chemistry, it is treated as a unique, singular entity requiring the definite article.

questionBank.question: The chart shows a rapid increase in ___ population of urban areas.
questionBank.explanation: Use 'the' when followed by 'of' specifying which population.

questionBank.question: In conclusion, ___ education plays a crucial role in society.
questionBank.explanation: Education is an uncountable concept being discussed generally.

questionBank.question: ___ education he received at that school was excellent.
questionBank.explanation: It is specific because it refers to the exact education 'he received at that school'.

questionBank.question: There is ___ European student in my speech class.
questionBank.explanation: Use 'a' because 'European' starts with a consonant sound ('y' sound), even though it starts with a vowel letter.

questionBank.question: Look at ___ moon tonight; it's incredibly bright.
questionBank.explanation: There is only one moon (in our sky), so we use the definite article.

questionBank.question: I usually have ___ breakfast at 7:00 AM.
questionBank.explanation: Routine meals (breakfast, lunch, dinner) do not take articles.

levels.title: Controlled Practice (L1 Traps)
levels.miniLesson:
⚠️ Level 2: The L1 Trap

Japanese has no articles, so students often guess and put "the" in front of general words. Never use "the" for general statements!

  • ❌ The international students face the culture shock.
  • ✅ ∅ International students face ∅ culture shock.

questionBank.question: ___ global warming is a major concern for scientists today.
questionBank.explanation: 'Global warming' is an uncountable noun being discussed as a general concept. No article is needed.

questionBank.question: ___ computers in the main lab are currently being updated.
questionBank.explanation: Even though it is plural, we use 'The' because we are talking about specific computers (the ones in the main lab).

questionBank.question: In modern society, ___ smartphones have changed how we communicate.
questionBank.explanation: We are speaking generally about all smartphones, so we use the zero article.

questionBank.question: ___ Japanese culture places a high value on social harmony.
questionBank.explanation: When discussing the general culture of a country, do not use 'the'.

questionBank.question: I am studying ___ history of the Japanese economic miracle.
questionBank.explanation: Because it is a specific history (of the economic miracle), we must use 'the'.

questionBank.question: ___ international students often experience homesickness during their first semester.
questionBank.explanation: General plural nouns (international students in general) take no article.

questionBank.question: ___ international students in my writing class are from Vietnam.
questionBank.explanation: We use 'The' because we are specifying exactly which students (the ones in my writing class).

questionBank.question: Many people believe that ___ technology is advancing too quickly.
questionBank.explanation: Technology is a general uncountable concept here, so it takes the zero article.

questionBank.question: We need to fix ___ technology in conference room B before the meeting.
questionBank.explanation: Specific technology (in conference room B) requires 'the'.

questionBank.question: ___ society must take responsibility for caring for the elderly.
questionBank.explanation: 'Society' used generally is an uncountable noun that takes no article.

questionBank.question: We are studying the differences between the US and ___ Japanese society.
questionBank.explanation: Even with an adjective (Japanese), general 'society' still takes no article.

questionBank.question: ___ nature provides us with the resources we need to survive.
questionBank.explanation: The abstract concept of 'nature' never takes an article.

questionBank.question: We enjoyed walking through ___ nature reserve near the university.
questionBank.explanation: Here, 'nature' is just an adjective describing a specific countable noun ('reserve').

questionBank.question: Governments should invest more money in ___ public transportation.
questionBank.explanation: Public transportation is an uncountable concept being discussed generally.

questionBank.question: They are complaining about ___ public transportation in Osaka.
questionBank.explanation: It is specific because it is limited to the transportation 'in Osaka'.

questionBank.question: ___ women live longer than men on average.
questionBank.explanation: General plural nouns ('women', 'men') take the zero article.

questionBank.question: ___ children need at least eight hours of sleep per night.
questionBank.explanation: General plural nouns ('children') take the zero article.

questionBank.question: Please tell ___ children to stop running in the hallway.
questionBank.explanation: This refers to specific children who are currently running in a specific hallway.

questionBank.question: ___ pollution is becoming a severe problem in many Asian cities.
questionBank.explanation: Pollution is a general uncountable noun.

questionBank.question: The city council is trying to reduce ___ pollution caused by factories.
questionBank.explanation: This is specific pollution (the pollution caused by factories).

questionBank.question: ___ freedom of speech is a fundamental human right.
questionBank.explanation: Abstract rights spoken of generally do not take an article.

questionBank.question: ___ English language has borrowed words from many other languages.
questionBank.explanation: While 'English' by itself takes no article, the phrase 'The [Language] language' always takes 'The'.

questionBank.question: My major is ___ sociology.
questionBank.explanation: Academic subjects never take an article.

questionBank.question: It is important to have ___ confidence when giving a presentation.
questionBank.explanation: Confidence is an abstract uncountable noun.

questionBank.question: ___ space exploration costs billions of dollars every year.
questionBank.explanation: General concepts like space exploration take the zero article.

questionBank.question: He wants to travel to ___ space someday.
questionBank.explanation: When referring to the universe outside Earth's atmosphere, 'space' takes no article.

questionBank.question: There isn't enough ___ space in this room for another desk.
questionBank.explanation: When referring to physical room/area, 'space' is uncountable and takes no article.

questionBank.question: I parked in ___ empty space near the entrance.
questionBank.explanation: Here, 'space' means a specific, countable parking spot, so it takes 'an' (because of 'empty').

questionBank.question: ___ crime has decreased over the last decade.
questionBank.explanation: General uncountable noun.

questionBank.question: The detective solved ___ crime quickly.
questionBank.explanation: Here it refers to a specific, countable criminal act.

levels.title: Application & Error Correction
levels.miniLesson:
📝 Level 3: The Essay Chain

Articles act like glue between sentences in your essays. Follow the First Mention → Second Mention rule:

New Idea (A/An) → Same Idea (The)
  • 🔵 The university built a new laboratory. (1st Mention)
  • 🟢 The laboratory features new equipment. (2nd Mention)

questionBank.question: Read the text and find the error: 'The government proposed a new law. A law will reduce pollution.'
options: Change 'a new law' to 'the new law'
questionBank.explanation: Once the law is introduced in the first sentence, it becomes specific. The second sentence must use 'The'.

questionBank.question: Read the text: 'She has a degree in the biology from the University of Tokyo.' Which phrase contains an article error?
questionBank.explanation: Academic subjects like 'biology' are uncountable concepts that do not take an article.

questionBank.question: Select the correct sentence:
options: The society needs to protect an environment.
Society needs to protect the environment.
A society needs to protect environment.
Society needs to protect an environment.
questionBank.correctAnswer: Society needs to protect the environment.
questionBank.explanation: 'Society' is general (no article), but 'the environment' is a unique, specific entity.

questionBank.question: Fill in the blanks: 'The city opened ___ new park. ___ park is located downtown.'
questionBank.explanation: First mention introduces 'a new park'. Second mention refers back to 'The park'.

questionBank.question: Find the error: 'Scientists have discovered a new species of bird. A bird lives in the rainforest.'
options: Change 'a new species' to 'the new species'
questionBank.explanation: The second sentence refers back to the specific bird introduced in the first sentence.

questionBank.question: Fill in the blanks: 'I read ___ interesting article yesterday. ___ article argued that ___ global warming is accelerating.'
questionBank.explanation: First mention (an), second mention (The), and general concept (no article for global warming).

questionBank.question: Find the error: 'He works as an engineer for the large tech company. The company is based in Tokyo.'
options: Change 'the large tech company' to 'a large tech company'
questionBank.correctAnswer: Change 'the large tech company' to 'a large tech company'
questionBank.explanation: When introducing the company for the first time, it should be 'a large tech company'.

questionBank.question: Select the most cohesive paragraph:
options: We face a major issue. An issue is poverty.
We face the major issue. The issue is poverty.
We face a major issue. The issue is poverty.
We face major issue. An issue is poverty.
questionBank.correctAnswer: We face a major issue. The issue is poverty.
questionBank.explanation: Introduce the singular countable noun with 'a', then refer back to it with 'The'.

questionBank.question: Fill in the blanks: '___ education is important, but ___ education I received in high school was lacking.'
questionBank.explanation: First 'education' is general (no article). Second 'education' is specific (the one I received).

questionBank.question: Find the error: 'A researcher conducted an experiment. An experiment's results were surprising.'
questionBank.explanation: Because the experiment was introduced in the first sentence, the second mention must be definite.

questionBank.question: Fill in the blanks: 'To write a good essay, you need ___ clear thesis statement and ___ strong evidence.'
questionBank.explanation: 'Thesis statement' is a singular countable noun (needs 'a'). 'Evidence' is an uncountable noun (needs no article).

questionBank.question: Find the error: 'The most people believe that the internet has improved communication.'
questionBank.explanation: When using 'most' to mean the majority of a general group, do not use 'The'.

questionBank.question: Select the correct sentence:
options: The life is full of surprises.
Life is full of the surprises.
A life is full of surprises.
questionBank.explanation: 'Life' and 'surprises' are both being used as general concepts here, so neither takes an article.

questionBank.question: Fill in the blank: '___ life of a student can be stressful during exams.'
questionBank.explanation: Because 'life' is specified by the phrase 'of a student', it becomes specific and needs 'The'.

questionBank.question: Find the error: 'He gave me an advice about my career.'
questionBank.explanation: 'Advice' is an uncountable noun and can never take 'a/an'.

questionBank.question: Find the error: 'They stayed at the hotel in Tokyo. A hotel was very expensive.'
options: Change 'the hotel' to 'a hotel' (first mention) and 'A hotel' to 'The hotel' (second mention)
questionBank.correctAnswer: Change 'the hotel' to 'a hotel' (first mention) and 'A hotel' to 'The hotel' (second mention)
questionBank.explanation: First mentions should be indefinite (a hotel). Second mentions must be definite (The hotel).

questionBank.question: Select the correct sequence: 'I bought ___ computer and ___ printer. ___ computer works well, but ___ printer is broken.'
questionBank.explanation: First mentions get 'a', second mentions get 'the'.

questionBank.question: Find the error: 'The poverty is a major issue in many developing countries.'
options: Change 'a major issue' to 'the major issue'
Change 'many developing countries' to 'the many developing countries'
questionBank.explanation: Poverty is an abstract, general concept and does not take 'The'.

questionBank.question: Fill in the blanks: '___ poverty in that specific region was caused by ___ drought.'
questionBank.explanation: Poverty is specific ('in that specific region'), so it takes 'The'. Drought is a singular countable event being introduced.

questionBank.question: Find the error: 'We had a terrible weather during our vacation.'
questionBank.explanation: 'Weather' is an uncountable noun. It cannot take 'a/an', even when preceded by an adjective.

questionBank.question: Select the correct sentence:
options: I need a time to think.
I need the time to think.
I need some time to think.
I need time to the think.
questionBank.correctAnswer: I need some time to think.
questionBank.explanation: 'Time' (in the sense of duration) is uncountable, so 'a time' is incorrect here.

questionBank.question: Find the error: 'She has a great knowledge of Japanese history.'
questionBank.explanation: Knowledge is uncountable. Even with an adjective, it does not take 'a'.

questionBank.question: Fill in the blanks: '___ history of ___ internet is fascinating.'
questionBank.explanation: It is a specific history (needs 'The'). The internet is a unique entity (needs 'the').

questionBank.question: Find the error: 'He is studying a medicine at the university.'
options: Change 'at the university' to 'at a university'
questionBank.explanation: Academic subjects and fields of study do not take 'a/an'.

questionBank.question: Select the correct sequence: 'There is ___ problem with ___ software. ___ problem causes the computer to crash.'
questionBank.explanation: Introduce the problem (a problem), specific software (the software), refer back to problem (The problem).

questionBank.question: Find the error: 'In the conclusion, I agree with the author.'
questionBank.explanation: The standard academic transition phrase is simply 'In conclusion' (no article).

questionBank.question: Fill in the blanks: '___ conclusion of ___ report was very critical.'
questionBank.explanation: Both are specific: the specific conclusion belonging to the specific report.

questionBank.question: Find the error: 'We are trying to find the solution to a problem we discussed earlier.'
options: Change 'to a problem' to 'to the problem'
questionBank.correctAnswer: Change 'to a problem' to 'to the problem'
questionBank.explanation: Because the problem was 'discussed earlier', it is known and specific, requiring 'the problem'.

questionBank.question: Fill in the blanks: '___ dogs make great pets, but ___ dog I have barks too much.'
questionBank.explanation: First 'dogs' is general (no article). Second 'dog' is specific to the speaker (the dog).

questionBank.question: Find the error: 'The air pollution in Beijing is improving, but a smog is still bad.'
questionBank.explanation: Smog is uncountable, so 'a smog' is impossible. It should be 'the smog' referring specifically to the smog in Beijing.

levels.title: IELTS/TOEFL Integration
levels.miniLesson:
📈 Level 4: Task 1 Data Rules

Examiners look for these exact collocations. Memorize them:

  • 📉 Trends: a significant increase, a dramatic drop
  • 📊 Data Points: the number of, the highest percentage
  • 🏔️ Peaks/Lows: reached a peak of, remained at the same level

questionBank.question: Between 2010 and 2015, there was ___ sharp decline in oil prices.
questionBank.explanation: Use 'a' with singular adjective-noun combinations describing trends (a sharp decline, a steady rise).

questionBank.question: The chart illustrates ___ proportion of students who study abroad.
questionBank.explanation: We use 'the' before 'proportion' or 'percentage' when describing a specific segment of a whole.

questionBank.question: In 2020, the sales figures reached ___ peak of 50,000 units.
questionBank.explanation: The correct academic collocation for Task 1 is 'reached a peak of'.

questionBank.question: There was ___ dramatic increase in the number of tourists visiting Kyoto.
questionBank.explanation: Noun phrases describing singular trends (increase, decrease, rise, fall) take 'a'.

questionBank.question: The data shows that ___ number of cars on the road doubled.
questionBank.explanation: Always use 'the number of' when referring to a specific count of items in a chart.

questionBank.question: Renewable energy experienced ___ period of stability between 2005 and 2008.
questionBank.explanation: The standard collocation is 'a period of [noun]'.

questionBank.question: The graph compares ___ amount of electricity produced by coal and wind.
questionBank.explanation: Always use 'the amount of' when referring to a specific uncountable quantity in a chart.

questionBank.question: Unemployment hit ___ lowest point in 2019.
questionBank.explanation: Superlatives (lowest, highest, most) always require 'the'.

questionBank.question: The population fell to ___ low of 2 million before recovering.
questionBank.explanation: The standard Task 1 collocation is 'hit a low of' or 'fell to a low of'.

questionBank.question: ___ majority of respondents preferred working from home.
questionBank.explanation: The standard phrase to describe more than 50% is 'The majority of'.

questionBank.question: After 2015, the figures remained at ___ same level for three years.
questionBank.explanation: The word 'same' is almost always preceded by 'the' (the same level, the same amount).

questionBank.question: There was ___ slight fluctuation in the price of gold.
questionBank.explanation: Like other trend nouns, 'fluctuation' takes 'a' when used as a singular noun phrase.

questionBank.question: The table illustrates ___ percentage of income spent on housing.
questionBank.explanation: Use 'the' when referring to a specific percentage shown in the data.

questionBank.question: To tackle this issue, ___ government should implement stricter laws.
questionBank.explanation: In IELTS Task 2, 'the government' is almost always used with 'the' because we assume each country has one specific ruling body.

questionBank.question: It is crucial to protect ___ environment for future generations.
questionBank.explanation: When referring to the natural world, 'the environment' always takes 'the'.

questionBank.question: Education plays ___ vital role in reducing poverty.
questionBank.explanation: The standard academic collocation is 'plays a role in'.

questionBank.question: This policy has ___ wide range of benefits for local communities.
questionBank.explanation: The standard phrasing is 'a wide range of' or 'a variety of'.

questionBank.question: On ___ other hand, some people argue that taxes are too high.
questionBank.explanation: The transition phrase is strictly 'On the other hand'.

questionBank.question: As ___ result of this policy, crime rates dropped.
questionBank.explanation: The standard transition phrase is 'As a result'.

questionBank.question: ___ internet has revolutionised the way we access information.
questionBank.explanation: 'The internet' is considered a unique, specific entity and always takes 'the'.

questionBank.question: There is ___ lack of funding for public schools in this district.
questionBank.explanation: The standard noun phrase is 'a lack of'.

questionBank.question: In ___ future, renewable energy will likely replace fossil fuels.
questionBank.explanation: The standard phrase is 'In the future'.

questionBank.question: Japan had ___ higher rate of recycling than the USA.
questionBank.explanation: When comparing rates using an adjective, use 'a' (a higher rate, a lower percentage).

questionBank.question: In 2010, ___ UK saw a minor decrease in unemployment.
questionBank.explanation: Countries with words like Kingdom, States, or Republic must take 'the' (The UK, The USA).

questionBank.question: Sales increased at ___ steady pace throughout the year.
questionBank.explanation: The standard phrase for describing speed/manner is 'at a [adjective] pace/rate'.

questionBank.question: The graph shows ___ upward trend in the use of public transport.
questionBank.explanation: Trend descriptions take a/an. 'Upward' starts with a vowel sound.

questionBank.question: This leads to ___ increase in global temperatures.
questionBank.explanation: Use 'an' before 'increase' when referring to it generally as a consequence.

questionBank.question: There was ___ overall upward trend across all three categories.
questionBank.explanation: Use 'an' because 'overall' starts with a vowel sound.

questionBank.question: By 2030, ___ population is expected to reach 9 billion.
questionBank.explanation: We use 'the' because it refers to the specific population of the area shown in the data.

questionBank.question: Both charts highlight ___ differences in consumer spending.
questionBank.explanation: Use 'the' because it refers to the specific differences shown in the charts.
